source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Solar energy at the rate $800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ strikes a flat solar panel for water heating. If the panel has absorptance $=0.96$ for all wavelengths and the sides are perfect insulators, calculate the maximum temperature of the water.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Solar energy at the rate $800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ strikes a flat solar panel for water heating. If the panel has absorptance $=0.96$ for all wavelengths and the sides are perfect insulators, calculate the maximum temperature of the water.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains PIL object representations rather than file paths or URLs
question text does not explicitly mention a figure, but raw_record.vision_relevance is "essential"
metadata.reasoning_chain:
The Stefan-Boltzmann law gives, under equilibrium, 
\[ 
\alpha \phi=\sigma T^{4} 
\] 
where $\sigma=5.67 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \, \mathrm{K}^{4}$ is Stefan's constant, $\phi$ is the solar energy flux density, and $\alpha$ is the absorptance of the panel. With 
\[ 
\begin{aligned} 
\sigma & =5.67 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \, \mathrm{K}^{4}, \\ 
\alpha & =0.96, \\ 
\phi & =800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}, 
\end{aligned} 
\] 
we obtain $T=341 \, \mathrm{K}$ for the maximum temperature of the water.

The Stefan-Boltzmann law gives, under equilibrium, 
\[ 
\alpha \phi=\sigma T^{4}

\[ 
\begin{aligned} 
\sigma & =5.67 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \, \mathrm{K}^{4}, \\ 
\alpha & =0.96, \\ 
\phi & =800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2},
